info.name: AM Platform
info.description:
Unified Postman collection for **am-platform** thin-layer services.

## Environments
| File | Use when |
|------|----------|
| `AM-Platform.local.postman_environment.json` | `npm run platform:dev` (localhost) |
| `AM-Platform.preprod.postman_environment.json` | Gateway at `am-dev.asrax.in/api` |

## Auto-capture (collection scripts)
**Pre-request:** fresh `idempotency_key` for meter/check POSTs; `X-Request-Id` header.

**Post-response:** saves `access_token`, `refresh_token`, `service_access_token`, `user_sub`, `subscription_id`, `plan_code`, `notification_id`, Google SSO vars.

## Modules
| Folder | Service |
|--------|--------|
| Identity | am-identity |
| Subscription | am-subscription |
| Notification | am-notification |

exec: * Runs before every request. Uses environment variables only (no hardcoded URLs).
// Ensure idempotency key exists for meter/check POSTs (regenerated per request below).
// Optional correlation header for platform services (ignored if unsupported).
* Auto-captures tokens and entity IDs into the active environment.
const text = typeof value === "string" ? value : String(value);
const data = body.data !== undefined ? body.data : null;
// Console summary for quick debugging in Postman runner.

item.name: Identity
item.description:
Organized API collection for **am-identity** (port 8113).

## Quick start
1. Import `AM-Identity.local.postman_environment.json`
2. Run **Health → Health Check**
3. Run **Auth → Login** (or Google SSO flow)
4. `access_token` / `refresh_token` are saved automatically
5. Run **Users → Get My Profile**

## Folders
| Folder | Purpose |
|--------|----------|
| 00 Health | Liveness |
| 01 Auth — Registration | Sign up |
| 02 Auth — Login & Session | Password login, refresh, logout |
| 03 Auth — Google SSO | URL + callback (manual browser step for `code`) |
| 04 Users | Bearer-protected profile/settings |
| 05 Internal | Service-account only |
| 99 Keycloak Helpers | Direct token calls for setup/debug |

## Gateway (future)
Public routes via Traefik: `/api/auth/*`, `/api/users/*`

request.description: Service liveness. Expect `{"status":"ok","service":"am-identity"}`.
Creates a user in Keycloak (admin API). Use a unique email per run.
Password grant via am-identity-service client. Saves `access_token` and `refresh_token`.
Exchange refresh token for new access token.
Revokes refresh token session. Returns 204.
Scaffolded — currently returns 501 until OTP flow is implemented.

item.name: 03 Auth — Google SSO
item.description:
**Flow:**
1. Run **Get Google Auth URL** → copy `auth_url` from response
2. Open URL in browser, sign in with Google
3. After redirect, copy `code` and `state` from callback query string
4. Set `google_auth_code` env var (or edit callback body)
5. Run **Google Callback** → tokens saved automatically

request.description: Returns Keycloak auth URL with `kc_idp_hint=google` and anti-replay `state`.
Exchange authorization code for AM tokens. `redirect_uri` must match the one used in step 1.
Returns profile from Keycloak userinfo + JWT roles.
Patches user attributes in Keycloak (settings stored as user attribute).

item.name: 05 Internal (Service Token Required)
item.description:
Requires a **service account** JWT (role: `service`).
Run **99 Keycloak Helpers → Client Credentials (am-identity-service)** first and use that token as `access_token`, or set `service_access_token` in environment.

request.description: Issues a machine token for downstream service calls.
Internal lookup stub. Set `user_sub` from login/profile response.

item.name: 99 Keycloak Helpers (Setup / Debug)
item.description: Direct Keycloak calls — useful when testing tokens without am-identity.

request.description: OpenID configuration document.
Machine token for internal endpoints. Set `identity_client_id` and `identity_client_secret` in environment.
Direct Keycloak password grant (public client). May be disabled depending on realm policy.

item.name: Subscription
item.description:
Organized API collection for **am-subscription** (port 8110).

## Quick start
1. Import `AM-Subscription.local.postman_environment.json`
2. Run **00 Health → Health Check**
3. Run **99 Keycloak Helpers → Password Login** (or login via AM Identity collection)
4. Run **02 Subscriptions → Get My Subscription**
5. For internal routes: **Client Credentials (am-portfolio-service)** then **03 Internal**

## Folders
| Folder | Purpose |
|--------|----------|
| 00 Health | Liveness |
| 01 Plans | Public plan catalog (Free / Pro / Premium) |
| 02 Subscriptions | User JWT — create, me, upgrade, pause, cancel |
| 03 Internal | Service token — entitlements, check, meter, bootstrap |
| 04 Webhooks | Lago provider webhook simulation |
| 99 Keycloak Helpers | Tokens for user + service-account flows |

## Gateway (future)
Public routes via Traefik: `/api/subscriptions/*`

request.description: Service liveness. Expect `{"status":"ok","service":"am-subscription"}`.

item.name: 01 Plans
item.description: Public plan catalog — no auth required.

request.description: Returns Free, Pro, Premium tiers from `lago-plans.json`.
Filter plans by billing interval.
Annual variants: am_pro_annual, am_premium_annual.

item.name: 02 Subscriptions (Bearer Required)
item.description: User-facing subscription lifecycle. Run **99 Keycloak Helpers → Password Login** first to set `access_token`.

request.description: Create or return existing subscription. Defaults to `am_free` if plan_code omitted. Saves `subscription_id`.
Current plan, limits, entitlements, and usage snapshot. Auto-creates free tier if missing.
Change plan (e.g. am_free → am_pro). Requires `subscription_id` from Get My Subscription.
Transition active → paused.
Transition paused → active.
Cancel subscription and sync with Lago.
Metered usage records for the authenticated user.

item.name: 03 Internal (Service Token Required)
item.description: For **am-gateway** and **am-portfolio-service**. Run **Client Credentials (am-portfolio-service)** first. Set `user_sub` to the Keycloak user id under test.

request.description: Ensure user has a subscription (defaults to free). Used by backend services on first login.
Full entitlement snapshot for gateway caching (60s TTL).
Gateway pre-flight check before document parse. Returns 429 if quota exceeded.
Feature-flag check (Pro/Premium only).
Idempotent usage record — buffers locally and syncs to Lago.
Gateway metering endpoint.

item.name: 04 Webhooks
item.description: Simulate Lago billing webhooks (no auth in dev).

request.description: Maps to `am.subscription.created.v1` platform event.
Maps to `am.subscription.suspended.v1` platform event.

item.name: 99 Keycloak Helpers (Setup / Debug)
item.description: Obtain JWTs for user and service-account flows.

request.description: OpenID configuration document.
User JWT for subscription routes. Saves `access_token` and `user_sub`.
Machine token for internal subscription routes (am-portfolio backend).
Machine token for API gateway entitlement checks.

item.name: Notification
item.description:
Organized API collection for **am-notification** (port 8111).

## Quick start
1. Import `AM-Notification.local.postman_environment.json`
2. Set `notification_client_secret` from `.secrets.env` (`AM_NOTIFICATION_CLIENT_SECRET`)
3. Run **00 Health → Ready Check**
4. Run **99 Keycloak → Client Credentials (am-notification-service)**
5. Run **03 Internal → Send Notification**
6. For user inbox routes: login via Identity collection first

## Folders
| Folder | Purpose |
|--------|----------|
| 00 Health | Liveness + readiness |
| 01 Inbox | User JWT — list, unread, mark read |
| 02 Preferences | User notification preferences |
| 03 Internal | Service token — synchronous send |
| 99 Keycloak Helpers | Machine tokens |
